# Contract: presets.ini Format

**Type**: Configuration file format contract
**Path**: `$SNAP_USER_COMMON/config/presets.ini`
**Generated by**: `se-llama.generate-presets` (invoked automatically on first run)
**Consumed by**: `llama-server --models-preset <path>` (native llama-server parsing)

---

## Purpose

`presets.ini` defines named model configurations for llama-server's router mode. Each
INI section is a named preset. The server is started without `--model`; clients select
which model/configuration to use by setting the `"model"` field in their API request to
the section name or alias.

llama-server parses this file natively — the snap wrapper does not parse it.

---

## Format

Standard INI. Section headers in `[brackets]` are preset names. Keys are CLI flag names
with leading `--` stripped (e.g., `n-gpu-layers` for `--n-gpu-layers`). Comments use
`;` or `#`.

Special sections:
- `[*]` — global defaults applied to all presets (merged before section-specific keys)
- `[default]` (or keys before any section header) — the default preset

```ini
; Global defaults applied to all presets
[*]
no-cache-prompt = true
cache-ram = 0
host = 127.0.0.1
port = 8080

; GPU-accelerated preset (Vulkan)
[phi3-mini]
model = /home/user/snap/se-llama/common/models/phi-3-mini-q4_k_m.gguf
alias = phi3-mini
n-gpu-layers = 99
ctx-size = 4096

; CPU-only preset (for machines without a Vulkan GPU)
[phi3-mini-cpu]
model = /home/user/snap/se-llama/common/models/phi-3-mini-q4_k_m.gguf
alias = phi3-mini-cpu
n-gpu-layers = 0
ctx-size = 4096
```

---

## Supported keys

Any llama-server CLI flag name (with `--` stripped) is valid. The most commonly used:

| Key | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `model` | filepath | Path to the GGUF model file for this preset |
| `alias` | string | API-visible model name (used in `"model"` field of requests) |
| `n-gpu-layers` | int | Layers to offload to GPU; `0` = CPU only, `99` = all |
| `ctx-size` | int | Context window tokens; `0` = use model metadata default |
| `no-cache-prompt` | bool | Disable cross-request KV accumulation |
| `cache-ram` | int (MiB) | In-memory cache size; `0` = disabled |
| `no-mmap` | bool | Disable model memory-mapping |
| `host` | string | Bind address (recommend `127.0.0.1`) |
| `port` | int | Listen port |

**Unknown keys are a fatal error** — llama-server exits with:
`"option '<key>' not recognized in preset '<name>'"`.
This differs from the earlier wrapper design; there is no pass-through of unknown keys.

The key `version` is silently skipped (reserved for future use by llama-server).

---

## Error behavior (all fatal at server startup)

| Condition | Error message |
|---|---|
| File missing | `"preset file does not exist: <path>"` |
| Malformed INI | `"failed to parse server config file: <path>"` |
| Unrecognized key | `"option '<key>' not recognized in preset '<name>'"` |

Named preset not found at **request time** → API error response (server keeps running).

---

## Security note

**`slot-save-path` MUST NOT appear in the shipped default `presets.ini`.** If a user
adds it to their config, llama-server will write KV cache data to the specified path.
This must be called out in the quickstart as the one mechanism that creates persistent
inference data on disk.
